target.title: [Bug]: Route-first commands accept --log-level but do not apply it
target.body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Route-first CLI commands accept the global `--log-level` option for routing, but do not apply it to the logging system because they bypass the Commander `preAction` hook that sets `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a current source checkout of OpenClaw main. Observed against `origin/main` around `dc09d148bb` / `c70b9849d9` (package version `2026.6.2`).
2. Run a route-first command with the documented global log-level option, for example:

   ```bash
   openclaw --log-level debug status
   ```

   The same route-first path covers common diagnostic commands such as `health`, `gateway status`, `agents list`, `config get`, `models list/status`, `tasks list/audit`, `channels list/status`, and `plugins list`.

### Expected behavior

`--log-level <level>` is documented as a global log level override for file + console output, so route-first commands should honor it the same way Commander-backed commands do.

### Actual behavior

The command is routed and runs, but the CLI log-level override is not applied on the route-first path.

### Source-level repro on current main

The root cause is visible without a packaged install:

- `src/cli/program/preaction.ts` reads the Commander root option and sets `process.env.OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL`.
- `src/cli/route.ts` handles route-first commands before full Commander parsing and did not set `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL`.
- `src/logging/logger.ts` / `src/logging/env-log-level.ts` resolve the effective logger level from `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL`.
- `src/cli/route.test.ts` already verified that `openclaw --log-level debug status` still routes, but did not assert that the log level takes effect.

### OpenClaw version

Current main/source checkout: package version `2026.6.2`; source-level behavior observed around `dc09d148bb` / `c70b9849d9`.

### Operating system

Observed by source inspection and local test coverage on Windows 11. The affected path appears platform-independent.

### Install method

Source checkout / local CLI development workflow.

### Model

N/A - CLI startup/logging behavior.

### Provider / routing chain

N/A - CLI startup/logging behavior.

### Additional provider/model setup details

N/A

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

Linked fix PR: #93460.

Related PR #93455 fixes a separate Commander-path issue where non route-first commands can reject a trailing root `--log-level`. This issue is intentionally separate: route-first commands bypass Commander entirely, so they can recognize `--log-level` enough to route while still failing to apply it.

### Impact and severity

Affected: users running route-first CLI commands with `--log-level`, especially for diagnostics.

Severity: Medium. This does not crash the CLI or corrupt data, but it silently ignores a documented diagnostic override on common fast-path commands.

Frequency: Expected to be deterministic for affected route-first commands.

Consequence: Debug/trace logging requested from the CLI may not be enabled when users need it for troubleshooting.

### Proposed fix

- Parse routed `--log-level` before route-first bootstrap.
- If every occurrence has a valid value, set `process.env.OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL` before `ensureCliExecutionBootstrap` / routed command execution, using the last valid value for Commander parity.
- If any value is missing or invalid, fall back to Commander so existing user-facing validation owns the error.
- Add focused coverage in `src/cli/route.test.ts` for prefix, suffix, repeated, invalid, and missing `--log-level` cases.

### Additional information

This issue should remain scoped to route-first log-level application. It is not about log rendering and should not absorb the Commander-path trailing root option normalization covered by #93455.
target.author: ooiuuii
